text: While a broad expert consensus has defined the core competencies required for education for sustainable development (ESD) in higher education, empirical data reflecting the perspectives of students and graduates remain scarce. This study addresses this gap by analyzing evaluation data from sustainable development curricula at the University of Bern. Through a standardized survey, students (N = 124), graduates (N = 121), and internship supervisors (N = 37) assessed the relevance of 13 specific competencies for both academic training and professional practice. The findings provide critical insights into how these stakeholders prioritize various skills, offering a practical evidentiary basis for refining ESD programs to better align with the demands of the labor market and the needs of learners.
